name: Caramel Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle 1/2 cup sugar in a shallow, heavy 3 1/2 quart Dutch oven; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar melts (sugar will clump) and syrup is light golden brown. Remove from heat.
2. Combine remaining 2 1/2 cups sugar and flour in a large saucepan, stirring well; add milk, and bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
3. Gradually pour about one-fourth of the hot mixture into caramelized sugar, stirring constantly; then add the remaining hot mixture (mixture will lump, but continue stirring until smooth).
4. Return the mixture to heat; cover and cook over low heat for 2 minutes. Uncover and cook, without stirring, over medium heat until a candy thermometer reaches 238 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Add butter, stirring to blend. Remove from heat, and cool, without stirring, until the temperature drops to 110 degrees Fahrenheit (about 1 hour). Add vanilla, and beat with a wooden spoon or at medium speed of an electric mixer, until of spreading consistency (about 20 minutes).
6. Yield: enough for a 2-layer cake.
